world-news.title: Trump says US to blockade ships crossing Strait of Hormuz
world-news.summary: US President Donald Trump announced that the US Navy will immediately blockade the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global shipping chokepoint through which approximately one-third of maritime-traded oil passes. This declaration represents a major shift in US military posture with significant implications for global energy markets and international commerce.

world-news.title: US-Iran ceasefire talks: What are the key sticking points?
world-news.summary: Ongoing negotiations between the United States and Iran have exposed deep divisions over ceasefire terms, prolonging uncertainty during a fragile two-week ceasefire period. The talks highlight fundamental disagreements on key issues that threaten to reignite regional conflict.

world-news.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
world-news.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced that Ukrainian forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks amid accusations from both sides of widespread ceasefire breaches. The escalating violations signal deteriorating prospects for a negotiated resolution to the conflict.

world-news.title: China says it will resume some ties with Taiwan after visit by opposition leader
world-news.summary: China announced plans to restore suspended diplomatic and economic ties with Taiwan, including direct flights and aquaculture imports, following a visit by the Beijing-friendly opposition leader of the self-ruled island. The move signals a potential thaw in cross-strait tensions following high-level political engagement.

world-news.title: Voters cast ballots in pivotal Hungarian election
world-news.summary: Hungarian voters are participating in elections that could see opposition leader Peter Magyar challenge Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has held power for 16 years and faced international criticism over democratic backsliding. The outcome will significantly impact the political direction of a key NATO and EU member state.

world-news.title: Germany's far-right AfD adopts 'radical' manifesto ahead of key polls
world-news.summary: Germany's far-right Alternative for Germany party has adopted a hardline manifesto ahead of crucial state elections in Saxony-Anhalt, where the party is leading in opinion polls. The development reflects growing extremist influence in one of Europe's largest democracies.

world-news.title: Peru's voters face choice of 35 contenders for ninth president in 10 years
world-news.summary: Peruvian voters are choosing among 35 presidential candidates amid a severe crisis of political instability, with the nation electing its ninth president in a decade during a period marked by surging violent crime and systemic corruption. The election reflects deep institutional dysfunction in a major Latin American economy.

world-news.title: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching Easter ceasefire
world-news.summary: Both Ukraine and Russia have accused the other of violating an Easter ceasefire agreement, with the Kremlin warning it will not extend the truce unless Kyiv accepts Russian terms. The breakdown signals potential escalation in the ongoing conflict between the two nations.

world-news.title: FIFA rejects Iran's request to relocate World Cup games amid US-Israel war
world-news.summary: FIFA has rejected Iran's request to relocate its World Cup fixtures from the United States amid ongoing regional conflict, according to Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum. The decision reflects FIFA's refusal to accommodate geopolitical concerns regarding Iran's safety and participation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: It begins: electric articulated haul trucks reach series production
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo has commenced regular production of its A30 and A40 Electric articulated haul trucks, marking the first time electric haulers of this scale have transitioned from pilot programs to serial production. This milestone represents a significant advancement in decarbonizing heavy-duty commercial transportation.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Workhorse slashes prices by $60,000 and Purolater buys 100
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Workhorse has reduced prices by approximately $60,000 on its W56 step van, prompting logistics operator Purolator to place a substantial 100-unit order. The price reduction reflects competitive pressure in the commercial EV market and demonstrates growing fleet adoption of electric vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Kia launches its most affordable EV at lower prices than expected
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kia has opened orders for its EV2 model in Europe at price points lower than initially anticipated, with first customer deliveries beginning next month. The launch of an ultra-affordable EV option strengthens Kia's competitive position in the mass-market electric vehicle segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla gets FSD Supervised approved in the Netherlands — here's what it means
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Dutch vehicle authority RDW has granted Tesla type approval for its Full Self-Driving Supervised system, making the Netherlands the first European country to officially approve the driver-assist technology. The approval follows more than 18 months of testing and is compliant with UN R-171 regulations for Driver Control Assistance Systems.

automotive-ev-battery.title: RAM 3500 driver switches to a Silverado EV, and the fuel savings are SHOCKING
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A real-world comparison between a 2023 RAM 3500 diesel truck and a 2026 Chevrolet Silverado EV demonstrates substantial fuel cost savings, providing practical evidence of the economic benefits of electric truck adoption. The comparison highlights the total cost of ownership advantage for commercial and personal fleet operators.

automotive-ev-battery.title: DHL Express Norway adopts Spirii charging platform
automotive-ev-battery.summary: DHL Express Norway has partnered with Danish charging solutions provider Spirii to optimize the charging infrastructure for its electric vehicle fleet. The partnership demonstrates enterprise fleet operators' commitment to deploying integrated charging management solutions across their EV operations.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 24 New EV Chargers Installed In Texas & Maine
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Love's Travel Stops has launched its first three EV charging stations in Texas, each equipped with four chargers featuring NACS connectivity, as part of broader expansion of public charging infrastructure across multiple states. The deployment targets major travel corridors to support long-distance EV travel.

automotive-ev-battery.title: The Chevy Equinox EV is 20% off right now with a $10,000 discount
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Chevrolet is offering a 20 percent discount on the Equinox EV, bringing prices down significantly on one of the market's most affordable electric vehicles. The promotional pricing reflects manufacturer efforts to accelerate EV adoption and clear inventory in a competitive market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Electric, diesel, or CNG? MOOG tech lets you pick all of the above, on just ONE machine
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MOOG has developed flexible powertrain technology that allows commercial fleets to operate diesel, natural gas, and battery-electric systems on a single machine, reducing transition costs and financial risk during decarbonization efforts. The approach enables phased fleet electrification strategies without complete vehicle replacement.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Boreal Orders 20 Candela Electric Ferries For Use In Norway
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Norwegian operator Boreal has ordered 20 electric fast ferries from Candela to replace its existing diesel-powered fleet, expanding electrification into maritime transportation. The large-scale order demonstrates commercial viability of electric ferry technology in European markets.

tech-ai.title: Nvidia-backed SiFive hits $3.65 billion valuation for open AI chips
tech-ai.summary: SiFive, an Nvidia-backed semiconductor company, has achieved a $3.65 billion valuation while developing open-source AI chip designs based on RISC-V architecture rather than proprietary x86 or ARM platforms. The funding round underscores growing industry interest in open-source alternatives for artificial intelligence hardware infrastructure.

tech-ai.title: The AI code wars are heating up
tech-ai.summary: Competition among tech giants intensifies as artificial intelligence systems become increasingly capable at writing and assisting with code development, a domain that has emerged as a critical battleground since Microsoft's early AI coding partnerships. The expansion reflects how AI-assisted programming has evolved from an experimental feature to a mainstream competitive advantage.

tech-ai.title: Californians sue over AI tool that records doctor visits
tech-ai.summary: Plaintiffs have filed a lawsuit challenging an artificial intelligence-powered medical transcription tool over allegations that it processes confidential patient-doctor conversations on external servers without proper consent. The case highlights growing privacy concerns regarding the use of AI systems in sensitive healthcare settings.

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman responds to 'incendiary' New Yorker article after attack on his home
tech-ai.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman published a blog post addressing both an apparent physical attack on his residence and an investigative New Yorker profile that questioned his trustworthiness and business practices. The response comes amid heightened scrutiny of the AI industry leader's conduct and credibility.

tech-ai.title: Your article about AI doesn't need AI art
tech-ai.summary: A New Yorker illustration accompanying a profile of Sam Altman was generated using artificial intelligence, prompting discussion about the appropriateness and ethics of using AI-created imagery in journalism, particularly when the subject matter involves AI itself. The incident raises questions about editorial standards and the potential conflicts inherent in using generative AI in media coverage of AI companies.

tech-ai.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here's a simple guide to common AI terms
tech-ai.summary: TechCrunch has compiled a comprehensive glossary defining key artificial intelligence terminology and concepts that have become prevalent in mainstream discourse as AI adoption accelerates. The resource clarifies essential vocabulary including large language models and common AI system limitations for readers seeking to understand industry discussions.

tech-ai.title: AI models are terrible at betting on soccer—especially xAI Grok
tech-ai.summary: A comparative analysis reveals that major artificial intelligence systems from Google, OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI perform poorly at predicting Premier League soccer outcomes, with xAI's Grok model showing particularly weak performance. The findings demonstrate limitations in AI systems' ability to process sports analytics and probabilistic reasoning.

tech-ai.title: Microsoft's "commitment to Windows quality" starts with overhaul of beta program
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft has initiated a comprehensive restructuring of its Windows Insider beta testing program to improve predictability and clarity for participants while maintaining the company's commitment to software quality. The overhaul aims to streamline how new features are tested and deployed to users.

tech-ai.title: Report: US demands Reddit unmask ICE critic, summons firm to grand jury
tech-ai.summary: The Trump administration has reportedly leveraged a federal grand jury to compel Reddit to identify a user who posted criticism of Immigration and Customs Enforcement, raising concerns about government overreach and online speech protections. The development highlights tensions between law enforcement authority and digital platform privacy practices.

tech-ai.title: Google says Polymarket bets showing up in News was an 'error'
tech-ai.summary: Google News briefly displayed prediction market betting odds from Polymarket alongside legitimate news content before removing the listings, with the company confirming the appearance violated editorial policies for eligible news sources. The incident illustrates ongoing challenges in content moderation and curation standards for news aggregation platforms.

finance-markets.title: Trump Says US Will Blockade Hormuz Strait After Iran Talks Fail
finance-markets.summary: President Donald Trump announced an immediate blockade of the Strait of Hormuz by the U.S. Navy following the collapse of peace negotiations with Iran in Islamabad. The decision represents a significant escalation in geopolitical tensions with direct implications for global oil markets and shipping routes.

finance-markets.title: Iran War Drives Oil Shock
finance-markets.summary: Escalating conflict in Iran is generating significant concerns about oil market volatility and potential supply disruptions affecting global energy prices. Analysts are monitoring whether current tensions will trigger a sustained supply crisis impacting broader markets.

finance-markets.title: Trump announces blockade of Strait of Hormuz as Vance leaves Pakistan talks with no deal. How financial markets are reacting.
finance-markets.summary: Following the failed U.S.-Iran peace negotiations, financial markets responded negatively with U.S. stock futures declining and oil prices rising sharply. The market reaction reflects investor concerns about geopolitical risk and energy supply disruption.

finance-markets.title: Earnings playbook: JPMorgan Chase and Netflix kick off the reporting season
finance-markets.summary: Major corporations including JPMorgan Chase and Netflix begin the first-quarter earnings season, a critical period for investors seeking corporate guidance amid ongoing economic uncertainty. The earnings reports are expected to provide insights into corporate health and market conditions.

finance-markets.title: Hormuz Tracker: Limited Transits Before Trump Announces Blockade
finance-markets.summary: Shipping traffic through the strategic Strait of Hormuz was already operating at reduced levels prior to President Trump's announcement of a U.S. Navy blockade. The situation reflects ongoing market tensions and supply chain concerns in one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints.

finance-markets.title: Hungarians vote in record numbers in election that could oust Orban and rattle Russia
finance-markets.summary: Hungarian voters turned out in record numbers for a landmark election that could potentially remove Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, whose populist "illiberal democracy" model has influenced authoritarian movements across Europe. The outcome has significant implications for European political stability and Russia's influence in the region.

finance-markets.title: Morgan Stanley predicts these beaten-down Chinese stocks can rebound on easing Middle East tensions
finance-markets.summary: Morgan Stanley strategists identify undervalued Chinese stocks with significant revenue exposure to the Middle East as potential rebound candidates if regional tensions ease. The analysis highlights how geopolitical developments in one region directly impact equity valuations in other markets.

finance-markets.title: Norway Averts Strike as Unions Clinch Wage Deal With Employers
finance-markets.summary: Norway's major trade unions reached a wage agreement with private-sector employers, averting a potential labor strike that could have disrupted energy production in western Europe's largest energy exporter. The deal supports market stability for European energy supplies.

finance-markets.title: Berkshire electric utility's court win could save it billions
finance-markets.summary: PacifiCorp, a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary, achieved a significant court victory that could reduce wildfire liability damages by $1 billion or more. The ruling provides substantial financial relief for the utility and its parent company.

finance-markets.title: Top Wall Street analysts prefer these dividend stocks for steady income
finance-markets.summary: Leading Wall Street analysts recommend dividend-paying stocks as a portfolio stabilization strategy amid persistent market uncertainty. These recommendations reflect investor appetite for stable income generation during volatile economic periods.

science-space.title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis II crew—astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, plus Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen—have successfully returned to Earth after completing a historic lunar flyby mission. The splashdown marks a major milestone in NASA's Moon-to-Mars exploration program, with the crew setting multiple spaceflight records during their journey.

science-space.title: Splashdown! Artemis 2 astronauts return to Earth after historic NASA mission to the moon
science-space.summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts completed their historic lunar mission and returned to Earth, achieving record-breaking spaceflight milestones. The successful return sets the stage for NASA's continued ambitious plans for lunar exploration and eventual crewed missions to the Moon's surface.

science-space.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: What's next for NASA after Artemis 2 moon success
science-space.summary: Following the successful Artemis 2 mission, NASA has outlined ambitious plans for future lunar exploration missions. The agency is preparing for Artemis 3 and subsequent missions that will advance human exploration of the Moon and support long-term deep space objectives.

science-space.title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
science-space.summary: The returning Artemis 2 astronauts have begun reflecting on their historic lunar mission, describing the profound personal impact of their journey around the Moon. Their firsthand accounts provide insight into the significance of this milestone achievement in human space exploration.

science-space.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
science-space.summary: Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL spacecraft successfully launched to the International Space Station carrying approximately 11,000 pounds of scientific equipment, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies. The cargo delivery continues NASA's critical logistics support for ongoing research and operations aboard the orbiting laboratory.

science-space.title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts
science-space.summary: Northrop Grumman's second Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft launched toward the International Space Station on Saturday, carrying over five tons of supplies for the station's crew. The successful launch demonstrates the continued operational success of commercial cargo resupply missions to support ISS operations.

science-space.title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
science-space.summary: The Artemis II crew captured striking imagery of Earth from the lunar far side during their historic mission around the Moon. These photographs provide a unique perspective of our planet as seen from humanity's nearest celestial neighbor.

science-space.title: New Perspective of Home
science-space.summary: During the Artemis II lunar flyby, the crew captured an unprecedented image showing the Moon and Earth aligned in the same frame, each partially illuminated by the Sun. The photograph demonstrates the remarkable visual perspective achieved during human lunar missions and the technical capabilities of modern spacecraft imaging systems.

science-space.title: Starstruck
science-space.summary: The Artemis II crew captured a detailed photograph of the Milky Way galaxy from lunar orbit on April 7, 2026, revealing the galaxy's spiral structure and the location of Earth within one of its arms. The image showcases the scientific value of conducting astronomical observations from locations beyond Earth's orbit.

science-space.title: From spa to boat party: 10 epic events for the Aug. 12, 2026, total solar eclipse in Spain and Iceland
science-space.summary: Multiple public engagement events have been planned across Spain and Iceland to commemorate the total solar eclipse occurring on August 12, 2026, offering opportunities for widespread scientific observation and public participation. These celebrations will allow communities along the path of totality to witness and study this significant astronomical event.

energy-climate.title: Iran war analysis: How 60 nations have responded to the global energy crisis
energy-climate.summary: One month into the US and Israel's conflict with Iran, at least 60 countries have implemented measures in response to the resulting global energy crisis. The geopolitical tensions are creating significant disruptions to energy markets and international energy security.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 10 April 2026: Worst energy crisis 'ever' | India withdraws COP33 bid | Drag artists and climate change
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers major climate and energy developments including an unprecedented energy crisis, India's withdrawal from hosting COP33, and ongoing climate policy debates. The briefing synthesizes key global developments affecting energy markets and climate policy.

energy-climate.title: Marine heatwaves 'nearly double' the economic damage caused by tropical cyclones
energy-climate.summary: Research shows that tropical cyclones intensifying over marine heatwaves become significantly more destructive, with economic damage nearly doubling compared to cyclones over normal ocean temperatures. This highlights the compounding climate impacts of ocean warming on extreme weather events.

energy-climate.title: India aims for 60% non-fossil power by 2035
energy-climate.summary: India has committed to achieving 60% non-fossil fuel in its power mix by 2035 as part of its energy transition strategy, supported by plans to install 500GW of renewable capacity by 2030. This represents one of the world's most ambitious renewable energy targets from a major developing economy.

energy-climate.title: India hits 150 GW solar milestone
energy-climate.summary: India has achieved 150 GW of cumulative solar capacity, with 44.6 GW of new PV capacity added in fiscal year 2026 according to JMK Research. This milestone reinforces India's position as a leading global solar power producer.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Record wind and solar saved UK from gas imports worth £1bn in March 2026
energy-climate.summary: Record wind and solar generation in the UK during March 2026 eliminated the need for gas imports valued at approximately £1 billion. The analysis demonstrates the economic benefits of renewable energy in reducing fossil fuel dependency and import costs.

energy-climate.title: Premier Energies begins trial production at 5.6GW solar facility in India
energy-climate.summary: Premier Energies has commenced trial production at its newly constructed 5.6GW solar module manufacturing facility in Telangana, India, significantly expanding domestic solar manufacturing capacity. This expansion strengthens India's solar supply chain and manufacturing capabilities.

energy-climate.title: Sodium-ion battery study claims zero thermal runaway breakthrough
energy-climate.summary: Chinese Academy of Sciences researchers have developed a polymerizable non-flammable electrolyte for sodium-ion batteries that reportedly eliminates thermal runaway risks while maintaining performance. This advancement could improve battery safety and accelerate adoption of sodium-ion technology as an alternative to lithium-ion.

energy-climate.title: South Korea sets 20% renewable power goal for 2030
energy-climate.summary: South Korea has established a target to increase renewable energy's share of electricity generation to at least 20% by 2030 as part of its energy transition strategy. This goal positions South Korea among developed nations raising renewable energy ambitions.

energy-climate.title: U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran tests solar supply
energy-climate.summary: The ongoing U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran presents potential supply chain risks for solar manufacturing in the Middle East, though most projects remain in early stages with the most immediate concerns centered on logistics disruptions. Export pricing volatility and shipment delays into the region could emerge if tensions persist.

health-biotech.title: Lilly launches GLP-1 pill, kicking off showdown with Novo Nordisk
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has launched Foundayo, an oral GLP-1 medication for obesity, directly competing with Novo Nordisk's injectable offerings and oral formulations. The launch represents a critical competitive moment in the rapidly growing obesity drug market, where Lilly aims to capture share despite Novo's three-month head start in the oral segment.

health-biotech.title: China biotech's CRISPR therapy suggests US drugmakers will face competition
health-biotech.summary: A Chinese-developed CRISPR gene therapy for beta thalassemia has demonstrated success in five patients who no longer require regular blood transfusions, signaling competitive pressure from China's biotech sector. The therapy is expected to be cheaper and potentially safer than existing alternatives, marking a significant advancement in CRISPR therapeutics availability.

health-biotech.title: STAT+: Replimune skin cancer drug that became FDA flashpoint is rejected again
health-biotech.summary: Replimune's oncolytic virus therapy for advanced melanoma has been rejected by the FDA for a second time, continuing a contentious regulatory matter. The repeated rejection underscores FDA concerns about the drug's clinical trial results despite the company's resubmission efforts.

health-biotech.title: Plan for remade ACIP panel adds focus on vaccine safety, matching Kennedy's push
health-biotech.summary: Following a court ruling suspending most Trump administration appointments, the CDC's vaccine advisory panel has published a new charter emphasizing vaccine safety and expanding member expertise requirements. The structural changes reflect broader political pressure to reshape vaccine oversight policies.

health-biotech.title: With 3 quick buyouts, Gilead leans into its latest transformation
health-biotech.summary: Gilead Sciences has executed a series of recent acquisitions focused on cancer and autoimmune conditions as part of a long-pursued diversification strategy. The multi-deal approach signals the company's commitment to expanding beyond its traditional antiviral portfolio.

health-biotech.title: Chasing a tough-to-treat lung disease, Avalyn plans an IPO
health-biotech.summary: Avalyn Pharmaceuticals, backed by prominent investors including Novo Holdings, is pursuing an IPO while developing improved treatments for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The company aims to advance therapies that address limitations in existing standard-of-care medicines.

health-biotech.title: China-based CAR-T company Oricell raises $40M more as it looks to go public
health-biotech.summary: Oricell Therapeutics has raised over $110 million in pre-IPO financing for its CAR-T cell therapy platform targeting solid tumors, including a GPC3-targeted therapy for liver cancer. The funding round positions the Shanghai-based company for a potential public offering.

health-biotech.title: Bayer pharma executive predicts US-Europe price spreads will narrow
health-biotech.summary: Bayer's chief operating officer for pharmaceuticals forecasts that pricing differentials between the U.S. and other markets will significantly narrow due to Trump administration pressure through "most favored nation" pricing initiatives. The prediction reflects anticipated regulatory shifts affecting global pharmaceutical pricing strategies.

health-biotech.title: Sidewinder secures $137M to advance 'precision' ADCs
health-biotech.summary: Sidewinder Therapeutics has secured $137 million in funding backed by OrbiMed and Novartis' venture arm to advance next-generation antibody-drug conjugate technology. The company is among developers working to expand the therapeutic reach of ADCs through precision approaches.

health-biotech.title: Invivyd reveals plans to test an antibody drug against measles
health-biotech.summary: Invivyd is developing an antibody-based therapeutic for measles positioned as a treatment alternative for vaccine-hesitant populations, amid a resurgence of measles cases at historically high levels. The approach represents a novel strategy to address infectious disease risks in resistant populations.

crypto-web3.title: Why North Korea keeps stealing billions in crypto — out in the open
crypto-web3.summary: North Korea's cryptocurrency theft operations have become increasingly sophisticated, representing a unique and dangerous threat to the crypto ecosystem distinct from other state-sponsored hackers. Security experts emphasize the need for the industry to understand the regime's distinct tactics and capabilities to better defend against future attacks.

crypto-web3.title: ECB backs EU plan to centralize crypto supervision under Paris-based ESMA watchdog
crypto-web3.summary: The European Central Bank has endorsed a proposal to consolidate cryptocurrency regulation under the European Securities and Markets Authority, while cautioning that adequate staffing and funding are essential. The ECB recommended a phased transition approach to minimize disruption to the market.

crypto-web3.title: Commodity traders are getting debanked due to Iran war, pushing them to rely on stablecoins
crypto-web3.summary: Traditional banks are withdrawing from trade finance due to Iran-related risk concerns, forcing commodity traders and non-bank lenders to increasingly turn to stablecoins for settlement operations. This shift reflects a growing mainstream adoption of cryptocurrency in response to geopolitical and regulatory pressures on traditional finance.

crypto-web3.title: 'Not Going to Stop at Bitcoin': Morgan Stanley Weighs Tokenization, Tax Solutions in Crypto Push
crypto-web3.summary: Morgan Stanley is expanding its cryptocurrency and blockchain strategy beyond Bitcoin, exploring tokenization opportunities and tax solutions for digital assets. The Wall Street firm signals that its crypto integration plans are still in early stages with significant growth potential ahead.

crypto-web3.title: Tron's Justin Sun slams Trump-backed WLFI for treating users as 'personal ATM' after $75 Million DeFi loan
crypto-web3.summary: Tron founder Justin Sun has publicly criticized World Liberty Financial, a Trump-family cryptocurrency venture, for allegedly exploiting users after it borrowed $75 million against its own token through a DeFi protocol. Sun's criticism is particularly significant given his previous substantial backing of the project.

crypto-web3.title: How Telegram plans to turn crypto into a global payments network
crypto-web3.summary: Telegram's TON blockchain is being developed as a cryptocurrency payments infrastructure integrated directly into the messaging platform to drive mainstream adoption. The article explores the platform's strategy and identifies the key opportunities for scaling crypto payments globally.

crypto-web3.title: There's a Way to Make Bitcoin Safe From Quantum Without a Fork, Researchers Say
crypto-web3.summary: Researchers have proposed a method to protect Bitcoin from future quantum computing threats using an alternative transaction design that operates within existing network rules. The approach avoids the need for a contentious network hard fork while addressing long-term security concerns.

crypto-web3.title: Super PAC tied to Tether makes first ad buy from firm founded by Tether's U.S. CEO
crypto-web3.summary: The Fellowship PAC, which has connections to Tether, spent $300,000 with Nxum Group, a firm co-founded by Bo Hines, the chief of Tether's U.S. operations and former Trump administration adviser. The transaction raises questions about potential conflicts of interest and the intersection of cryptocurrency and political spending.

crypto-web3.title: New Tools Aim to Make AI 'Vibe Coding' Safer for Crypto
crypto-web3.summary: Matterhorn and the ASI Alliance have launched new auditing tools and safety mechanisms to improve the security of AI-generated smart contracts in cryptocurrency development. The initiative addresses growing concerns about the reliability and security risks of machine learning-assisted code generation in blockchain applications.

crypto-web3.title: Trump token sees whale accumulation ahead of Mar-a-Lago gala; senators raise questions over event
crypto-web3.summary: Large holders of the TRUMP token have transferred substantial amounts to private wallets in advance of a Mar-a-Lago fundraising event, prompting inquiry from U.S. senators. The activity raises regulatory questions about potential market manipulation and the governance of politically-tied cryptocurrencies.

startups-vc.title: 20VC x SaaStr: Anthropic Hit $30 Billion and Training Costs a Quarter of OpenAI's, SpaceX Files at $1.75 Trillion, and Why the Big Three Will Dwarf Every IPO of the Last 25 Years Combined
startups-vc.summary: Anthropic has achieved $30 billion in annualized revenue in just five years, a trajectory that significantly outpaces historical benchmarks like Salesforce's 25-year journey to the same milestone. The impending IPOs of Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are projected to create more aggregate value than all VC-backed IPOs since 2000 combined, fundamentally reshaping the venture capital landscape.

startups-vc.title: The Big 3 IPOs Will Dwarf Everything Else Since 2000. Combined.
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are preparing for public offerings that are projected to collectively exceed the value of all VC-backed IPOs from the past 25 years. OpenAI's recent $122 billion funding round represents the largest private capital raise in Silicon Valley history, underscoring the scale of these upcoming listings.

startups-vc.title: North America Q1 Funding Surges Across Stages To Record Level
startups-vc.summary: U.S. and Canadian venture funding reached $252.6 billion in seed through growth-stage rounds during Q1 2026, more than tripling the prior quarter and marking the largest quarterly total on record. This unprecedented surge demonstrates robust investor confidence across all funding stages in the region.

startups-vc.title: This Is A Momentous Year For Early-Stage Unicorns
startups-vc.summary: Forty-seven seed- and early-stage companies achieved unicorn status in Q1 2026 alone, positioning 2026 to deliver the largest cohort of young unicorns in history. This trend reflects the dramatic acceleration of value creation among emerging startups in the current venture ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: Sierra's Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
startups-vc.summary: Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent-as-a-service platform designed to replace traditional click-based applications by enabling users to describe tasks in natural language for autonomous agent execution. This development represents a significant shift toward AI-powered automation in enterprise software.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: SiFive Leads With $400M For Custom Chip Designs As Aviation, Biotech And Defense Startups Also Raise Big
startups-vc.summary: SiFive secured a $400 million funding round for custom chip design while startups across semiconductors, aerospace, and biotech raised substantial capital. The diverse range of industries participating in large funding rounds reflects investor appetite across multiple high-growth sectors.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Largest Financings Went To Defense, Wearables, Energy And Security
startups-vc.summary: Saronic, an Austin-based developer of autonomous vessels, raised $1.75 billion in Series D funding, leading this week's largest financing rounds across defense, wearables, energy, and security sectors. The financing activity underscores continued investment momentum in infrastructure and technology-driven industries.

startups-vc.title: Fintech Startups Globally Raise More Money In Far Fewer Deals In Q1 2026
startups-vc.summary: Global fintech startups raised $12 billion across 751 deals in Q1 2026, a 5% increase in capital despite a 31% decline in the number of deals compared to the same period in 2025. This trend indicates consolidation of funding toward fewer, likely better-capitalized fintech ventures.

startups-vc.title: Databricks: Only 19% of Organizations Have Deployed AI Agents. But They're Already Creating 97% of Databases.
startups-vc.summary: Databricks' State of AI Agents 2026 report, drawn from 20,000+ organizations including over 60% of the Fortune 500, reveals that while AI agent adoption remains at 19%, these tools are already generating 97% of new database content. This data highlights the transformative but still-early adoption phase of AI agents in enterprise environments.

startups-vc.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna's layoffs and recovery
startups-vc.summary: Anjuna Security, a venture-backed cybersecurity startup that scaled to 75 employees in 2021 amid hypergrowth expectations, faced significant challenges when market conditions shifted in 2022. The case study offers valuable lessons on hiring discipline and adapting to market realities for founders navigating economic cycles.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers have exploited known vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users across more than 18,000 networks. The sophisticated campaign enabled large-scale credential theft without deploying malware, posing significant risks to enterprise security infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: U.S., Canadian, and German authorities successfully dismantled four major botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad) responsible for record-breaking distributed denial-of-service attacks that compromised over three million IoT devices. The coordinated international enforcement action disrupted infrastructure capable of launching devastating attacks against critical targets.

cybersecurity.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
cybersecurity.summary: An Iran-linked hacktivist group claimed responsibility for a data-wiping attack against medical device manufacturer Stryker, forcing the company to send home over 5,000 workers at its Irish headquarters and triggering building emergencies at U.S. facilities. The attack highlights the vulnerability of critical healthcare infrastructure to state-sponsored cyber operations.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes "UNKN," Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: German authorities identified and exposed 31-year-old Russian national Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin as "UNKN," the mastermind behind ransomware gangs GandCrab and REvil responsible for at least 130 cybercrimes and extortion attacks. The identification represents a significant law enforcement victory in disrupting major ransomware operations that targeted victims across Europe.

cybersecurity.title: 'Starkiller' Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
cybersecurity.summary: A sophisticated phishing-as-a-service platform called Starkiller bypasses traditional anti-phishing defenses by proxying legitimate login pages and relaying credentials in real-time, including multi-factor authentication tokens. The advanced service represents a significant escalation in phishing attacks, making traditional detection methods substantially less effective.

cybersecurity.title: EngageLab SDK Flaw Exposed 50M Android Users, Including 30M Crypto Wallet Installs
cybersecurity.summary: A patched vulnerability in the widely-used EngageLab SDK exposed millions of Android users to unauthorized access of private data, with particular risk to approximately 30 million cryptocurrency wallet installations. The flaw allowed applications to bypass Android security sandboxes, potentially compromising sensitive financial and personal information.

cybersecurity.title: Marimo RCE Flaw CVE-2026-39987 Exploited Within 10 Hours of Disclosure
cybersecurity.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in the open-source Marimo Python notebook was actively exploited within 10 hours of public disclosure, according to Sysdig research. The vulnerability with a CVSS score of 9.3 affects all versions prior to and including the current release, creating urgent patching requirements for affected users.

cybersecurity.title: Backdoored Smart Slider 3 Pro Update Distributed via Compromised Nextend Servers
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors compromised the update infrastructure for the Smart Slider 3 Pro plugin to distribute a backdoored version, impacting the widely-deployed plugin with over 800,000 active installations across WordPress and Joomla. The supply chain attack allowed malicious code injection to reach hundreds of thousands of websites through legitimate update channels.

cybersecurity.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
cybersecurity.summary: The ongoing GlassWorm campaign has evolved to employ a new Zig-based dropper targeting integrated development environments, discovered through a malicious Open VSX extension masquerading as the legitimate WakaTime activity tracker. The attack methodology allows adversaries to stealthily compromise developer machines and potentially gain access to sensitive source code and credentials.

cybersecurity.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
cybersecurity.summary: Hungarian and El Salvadoran government agencies, along with multiple U.S. law enforcement departments, have utilized Webloc, an advertising-based geolocation surveillance system developed by Israeli company Cobwebs Technologies. The tool's widespread adoption by law enforcement raises privacy concerns regarding mass surveillance capabilities and the tracking of 500 million devices through location data.

asia-pacific.title: Why China is looking to Central Asia as Middle East grows riskier
asia-pacific.summary: China is reassessing its energy security strategy amid Middle East instability, with the Iran conflict exposing vulnerabilities in its heavy reliance on maritime imports through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint carrying approximately one-fifth of global oil and gas. The disruption is prompting Beijing to diversify its energy sourcing toward Central Asia to mitigate supply chain risks.

asia-pacific.title: Trump orders US Navy to block Hormuz after Iran talks fail
asia-pacific.summary: Following the collapse of US-Iran peace talks, President Trump has ordered the US Navy to establish a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a move that has prompted Iran's Revolutionary Guards to warn they maintain full control of the waterway and would counter any military action. The escalation significantly heightens geopolitical tensions in a critical global energy corridor.

asia-pacific.title: Trump faces a dilemma in the aftermath of failed US-Iran peace talks: Chinese analysts
asia-pacific.summary: The breakdown of US-Iran peace negotiations in Islamabad has left President Trump facing difficult political and strategic choices regarding potential military escalation, with midterm elections creating domestic political constraints. Chinese analysts note that continued inaction risks perpetuating Strait of Hormuz access disputes while military action could prove electorally damaging.

asia-pacific.title: Friendly Strangers: How Two Peoples Across the Taiwan Strait Are Drifting Apart
asia-pacific.summary: Populations across the Taiwan Strait are experiencing structural divergence that will prove difficult to reverse, reflecting deepening societal and political differences between China and Taiwan. This demographic and cultural drift carries significant implications for cross-strait relations and long-term stability.

asia-pacific.title: The Quiet Quad: From Strategic Signalling to Embedded Minilateralism – or a Silent Drift?
asia-pacific.summary: The Quad grouping, previously known for high-profile strategic summitry, has entered a period of marked quietude that contrasts sharply with its recent elevated profile. The shift raises questions about whether the arrangement is evolving into embedded minilateral cooperation or experiencing strategic drift.

asia-pacific.title: China-US Trade Relations: Between Engagement and Decoupling
asia-pacific.summary: Survey findings indicate that US-China economic policy presents more nuanced options than a simple binary choice between full engagement and economic decoupling. The research complicates conventional narratives about the trajectory of bilateral trade relations.

asia-pacific.title: The Middle Corridor in an Era of Supply Chain Disruption
asia-pacific.summary: The Middle Corridor trade route has demonstrated its strategic value during periods of global supply chain disruption, positioning it as a potential alternative to traditional shipping lanes. The article examines whether this route can develop into a primary artery for Eurasian trade.

asia-pacific.title: Progress hailed as Hong Kong, Shanghai link digital authentication systems
asia-pacific.summary: Hong Kong and Shanghai have advanced their financial integration by connecting digital authentication systems, with five mainland enterprises establishing operations in Hong Kong as part of deepening digital cooperation. The collaboration was reviewed during the first annual meeting of the Shanghai-Hong Kong Digital Cooperation Working Group.

asia-pacific.title: Hungarians vote in record numbers in election that could oust Orban, rattle Russia
asia-pacific.summary: Hungary is experiencing record voter turnout in an election that could remove Prime Minister Viktor Orban from power following three years of economic stagnation and rising living costs. The outcome carries potential implications for European geopolitics and Russia's regional influence.

asia-pacific.title: Hamas to meet Egyptian mediators over alleged Gaza truce violations by Israel
asia-pacific.summary: Hamas is convening with Egyptian mediators to address mutual accusations of ceasefire violations between Israeli and Palestinian forces in Gaza, where a US-backed truce has substantially reduced the two-year conflict affecting 2.2 million residents. The mediation efforts aim to clarify compliance with truce terms.
